items.title: Armenia’s defiant drag scene braves pushback
items.description: Armenia sits at the crossroads of Europe, Asia and the Middle East. While in much of the European Union, the drag scene is mainstream, in Armenia it remains underground. Performers face threats of violence, stigma, police pressure and even rejection by their own families. Despite this, drag queens have built a vibrant, bustling community that has attracted both local Armenians and international crowds. They make up a passionate and determined microculture challenging ultraconservative norms. And this avant garde scene is, in turn, being influenced by Armenia’s evolving political landscape.

items.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on February 28, 2026, in joint precision strikes by the US and Israeli militaries. The airstrikes killed Khamenei as well as other members of his family.

items.title: Haitian medical students demand relocation of teaching hospital in capital
items.description: The institution in gang-controlled downtown Port-au-Prince plays a vital role in clinical training for young doctors.

items.title: France to face Paraguay as World Cup last 16 gets under way
items.description: France are bracing for furnace-like temperatures when they face Paraguay in Philadelphia in the World Cup's last 16 on Saturday as co-hosts Canada prepare for a daunting date with African giants Morocco in Houston. With a brutal heatwave roasting swathes of the United States as it celebrates July 4th, Les Bleus take on Paraguay at Lincoln Financial Field against a backdrop of extreme heat warnings.

items.title: Fermi CEO Neugebauer suspends proxy campaign after judge’s recusal
Iran's slain supreme leader lying in state in Tehran head of funeral events
items.description: Nearly four months after his death, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's burial is taking place as Iran and the US observe a fragile ceasefire.

items.title: Javier Milei taps lifetime politician Diego Santilli to lead his Cabinet after scandal
items.description: Javier Milei once vowed to eradicate entrenched politicians like Diego Santilli, yet he turned to him as a candidate and later named him interior minister. Now, the former PRO politician is Argentina's new Cabinet chief. Leer más

items.title: Buenos Aires City poverty rate rises after five quarters of decline
items.description: Over 651,000 porteños – more than one in five residents – were living in poverty in the first quarter of 2026, according to the IDECBA city statistics bureau. Leer más

items.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
items.description: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

items.title: First batch of 273 Ugandans arrives from South Africa amid unrest
items.description: Uganda has launched the first phase of a large-scale evacuation of its citizens from South Africa, bringing home 273 nationals amid rising anti-illegal immigration protests and xenophobic violence. More charter flights are planned as hundreds more Ugandans seek voluntary repatriation.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: Uba Sani flags off erosion control project, pays N2bn compensation
items.description: Governor Sani distributed cheques of about N2 billion to 1, 000 individuals whose houses will be affected. The project stretches from Rigasa to Tudun Wada wards in Igabi and Kaduna South local governments respectively The project stretches from Rigasa to Tudun Wada wards in Igabi and Kaduna South local governments respectively The post Uba Sani flags off erosion control project, pays N2bn compensation appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce in NYC ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler
items.description: The star-studded celebration saw several streets in Midtown Manhattan shut, as hordes of fans gathered outside the arena.

items.title: Santilli sworn in as Cabinet chief as Milei reshuffles pack
items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: Argentina’s beef export boom to United States leaves locals paying more for steak
items.description: Argentina’s exports to the United States are expanding fast past premium steaks to lower-quality cuts. That's great for exporters, but worse for locals, who see prices becoming less affordable. Leer más

items.title: Argentina's Nico Paz happy to extend Como loan say Real Madrid
items.description: Real Madrid have given Argentina star Nico Paz permission to extend his loan deal with Italian side Como. Leer más

items.title: How fake agency ‘DG’ Adeyemi outsmarted federal officials to secure CBN accounts, office space
items.description: Relying on letters from Mr Adeyemi, the OSGF and OAGF processed requests for an office space and CBN accounts for his non-existent agency. The post How fake agency ‘DG’ Adeyemi outsmarted federal officials to secure CBN accounts, office space appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Milei-linked Fundación Faro told to name donors behind US$4.8mn windfall
items.description: Fundación Faro presented itself as a platform of ideas. But the libertarian think tank’s opaque finances, links to key Milei insiders and 400-fold surge in donations have left the organisation facing uncomfortable questions from regulators and judges. Leer más

items.title: US marks 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers and extreme weather
items.description: US President Donald Trump's speech included some of his political agenda but also honoured war veterans and American history.

items.title: Mali creates state body to regulate gold trade
items.description: Mali has created a new state agency to regulate its booming artisanal gold sector after uncovering major discrepancies between officially declared exports and imports reported abroad.

items.title: Anguished families left to identify Venezuela quake victims at makeshift morgue
items.description: The disaster has overwhelmed local services, with bodies put outside or in tents for identification.

items.title: Nurse says psychiatrist downplayed Maradona's crisis during home hospitalisation
items.description: Diego Maradona suffered a sudden change in mood that was downplayed by his psychiatrist during the period of home hospitalisation in which he died, claims nurse in testimony. Leer más

items.title: Egypt makes history, heartbreak for Cape Verde at World Cup
items.description: Heartbreak for Cape Verde as the World Cup debutants bowed out after a thrilling 3-2 extra-time defeat to defending champions Argentina. The Blue Sharks twice battled back to level the match, pushing Lionel Messi's side to the limit in one of the tournament's biggest surprise performances.

items.title: South Africa deploys troops as anti-migrant protests escalate
items.description: South Africa has deployed more than 3,000 soldiers across the country to reinforce security as anti-migrant protests intensify, with organisers vowing to stage demonstrations every week.

items.title: More Chinese investment good for wider relationship, says India’s envoy to China
items.description: Noting that trade is expanding but lopsided, Ambassador Vikram Doraiswami pitches for more Indian exports to China, especially in areas such as pharmaceuticals where India is globally competitive

items.title: China launches coast guard patrol east of Taiwan despite international pushback
items.description: This is the second time in roughly a month that China, which views democratically ‌governed Taiwan as its own territory, has sent coast guard ships into the waters off Taiwan’s east coast

items.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter'
items.description: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.
items.source: Europe's new climate in seven charts

items.title: US Ambassador Peter Lamelas: ‘United States stands shoulder to shoulder with Argentina’
items.description: US Ambassador to Argentina Peter Lamelas proclaims support for President Javier Milei and explains his own "America Dream" in a speech marking 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence. Leer más

items.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
items.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church detained in China in October has been released, according to rights advocates
